news_summary: A Ukrainian doctor has successfully rebuilt her life from Kharkiv to the UK’s health service, exemplifying resilience and dedication to helping others.
news_summary_fr: Une médecin ukrainienne a réussi à se reconstruire une vie, de Kharkiv jusqu’au système de santé britannique, incarnant ainsi la résilience et le dévouement au service des autres.
news_summary_es: Una médica ucraniana ha logrado rehacer su vida tras su traslado de Járkov al sistema sanitario del Reino Unido, lo que constituye un ejemplo de resiliencia y dedicación al servicio de los demás.

articles.title: From Kharkiv to the UK’s health service: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her life – and helps others do the same
articles.summary: On 23 February 2022, in her home city of Kharkiv, Dr Inna Soldatenko finished work, collected her daughter from school, cooked dinner and prepared a lecture for her students. The next morning, she awoke to explosions.
articles.content:
It was the day Russian forces launched their full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Inna lost the life she had spent decades building - her career as a consultant rheumatologist, her home and her sense of safety.

Three years on, as the war continues in Ukraine, and bombs still go off in Kharkiv, she is practising medicine again in London and helping other refugee health workers find their footing in the UK.

“Before the war, I thought I’d reached everything I wanted,” she said. “I had my career, my family, my home. There was never any thought of leaving.”

© Inna Soldatenko

Inna’s story traces the journey of one of more than 260,000 Ukrainians who found safety in the United Kingdom following the invasion - a journey marked by loss, resilience and the transformative power of welcome.

It also highlights what happens when skilled refugees are given the chance to contribute.

Escaping the war with help

When the war broke out, and as fighting intensified and buildings around them were destroyed, Inna and her family - her two daughters, her parents and her cat - fled Kharkiv with only a few documents and belongings, thinking that they would return home within a couple of days.

Driving for more than 26 hours through Ukraine, Moldova and Romania, the family were welcomed by strangers sharing food, safety and kindness.

“I still remember them,” she said of the volunteers who chose to help. “Like part of the family.”

After travelling through Bulgaria and reconnecting with friends she had met years earlier in London, Inna arrived in the UK in May 2022 through the Homes for Ukraine scheme, a community-led initiative that allows people across the UK to sponsor Ukrainians fleeing the war by offering safe accommodation and the chance to rebuild their lives.

© Humanitarian Mission Proliska/Jose Colon

Starting over, one step at a time

Like many people forced to flee to another country, Inna faced barriers to returning to her profession, including language challenges and lack of recognition of her qualifications.

She began in an administrative role with the Lewisham and Greenwich Trust of the UK’s National Health Service, NHS, in southeast London.

Her NHS colleagues became what she calls her ‘work family’. They helped with her English, built her confidence and encouraged her to sit the exams needed to practise as a doctor in the UK.

“They believed in me much more than I believed in myself,” she said.

She passed all the required exams and returned to rheumatology, her chosen specialty, providing care directly to patients once more.

Of the more than 260,000 Ukrainians who have arrived in the UK since the invasion, over 60,000 are now employed across different sectors of the economy.

Building a network for refugee health workers

Inna’s journey did not stop there. Working alongside other displaced health-care professionals, she helped establish the Ukrainian Medical Charity, a national network supporting refugee doctors, nurses and health workers to access NHS employment opportunities.

Her advocacy has also helped shape the NHS Refugee Employment Programme, which assists refugees from many backgrounds into roles across the UK health service.

“Refugees want to give back to this country, and we have a duty to welcome them as well,” said Kathleen, a rheumatology clinical nurse specialist and Inna’s colleague.

Today, Inna balances work and family life in London “just like an ordinary person”, she says. What stays with her most is what she experienced in different countries, new neighbourhoods and hospital corridors: the power of welcome.

“When you're forced to flee, it’s like your roots are cut,” Inna says. “The NHS and the people around me helped me grow new ones. That stability, that kindness, it changes everything - like the sunshine on your garden.”
articles.source: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
articles.reasoning: The article tells the inspiring story of a Ukrainian refugee doctor who rebuilt her life and career in the UK, highlighting the positive impact of refugee integration and support programs. It demonstrates resilience, community support, and meaningful contributions of refugees to society, with broad relevance and substantial detail.
articles.personality_title: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her career and helps others within the UK health service
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – In February 2022, Russian forces invaded Ukraine, forcing many people to flee their homes. Among them was Dr Inna Soldatenko, a rheumatologist from Kharkiv, who lost her career and sense of safety overnight.

**What happened** – After escaping Ukraine with her family, Inna arrived in the UK in May 2022 through the Homes for Ukraine scheme, which connects refugees with people offering shelter. She started working in an administrative role in the National Health Service (NHS) while improving her English and preparing for medical exams. With support from NHS colleagues, she passed the exams and returned to practicing as a doctor in London.

**Impact** – Inna’s story shows how skilled refugees can rebuild their lives and contribute to their new communities. She helped create the Ukrainian Medical Charity to support other refugee health workers and influenced a wider NHS program that helps refugees find jobs in healthcare. More than 60,000 Ukrainians in the UK now work in various sectors, proving the value of welcoming refugees.

**What's next step** – Inna continues to balance her medical work and family life while supporting other refugee professionals. The NHS Refugee Employment Programme is expected to grow, offering more refugees chances to work in healthcare across the UK.

**One-sentence takeaway** – With support and determination, a Ukrainian doctor rebuilt her career in the UK and now helps other refugee health workers do the same.
articles.personality_title_fr: Une médecin ukrainienne reconstruit sa carrière et aide d'autres réfugiés dans le système de santé britannique
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – En février 2022, l'invasion russe de l'Ukraine a forcé de nombreuses personnes à fuir leur domicile. Parmi elles, la docteure Inna Soldatenko, rhumatologue à Kharkiv, a perdu sa carrière et son sentiment de sécurité du jour au lendemain.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Après avoir fui l'Ukraine avec sa famille, Inna est arrivée au Royaume-Uni en mai 2022 grâce au programme Homes for Ukraine, qui met en relation les réfugiés avec des personnes offrant un hébergement. Elle a commencé par un poste administratif au sein du National Health Service (NHS), tout en améliorant son anglais et en préparant ses examens médicaux. Soutenue par ses collègues du NHS, elle a réussi les examens et a repris la pratique médicale à Londres.

**Impact** – L'histoire d'Inna montre comment des réfugiés qualifiés peuvent reconstruire leur vie et contribuer à leur nouvelle communauté. Elle a aidé à créer la Ukrainian Medical Charity pour soutenir d'autres professionnels de santé réfugiés et a influencé un programme plus large du NHS aidant les réfugiés à trouver un emploi dans le secteur de la santé. Plus de 60 000 Ukrainiens travaillent aujourd'hui dans divers secteurs au Royaume-Uni, prouvant l'importance d'accueillir les réfugiés.

**Prochaine étape** – Inna continue de concilier travail médical et vie de famille tout en soutenant d'autres professionnels réfugiés. Le programme d'emploi pour réfugiés du NHS devrait se développer, offrant plus d'opportunités dans le secteur de la santé à travers le Royaume-Uni.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Grâce au soutien et à sa détermination, une médecin ukrainienne a reconstruit sa carrière au Royaume-Uni et aide désormais d'autres professionnels de santé réfugiés à faire de même.
articles.personality_title_es: Doctora ucraniana reconstruye su carrera y ayuda a otros refugiados en el sistema de salud del Reino Unido
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – En febrero de 2022, las fuerzas rusas invadieron Ucrania, obligando a muchas personas a huir de sus hogares. Entre ellas estaba la doctora Inna Soldatenko, reumatóloga de Járkov, quien perdió su carrera y su sensación de seguridad de un día para otro.

**Qué pasó** – Tras escapar de Ucrania con su familia, Inna llegó al Reino Unido en mayo de 2022 a través del programa Homes for Ukraine, que conecta a refugiados con personas que ofrecen alojamiento seguro. Comenzó trabajando en un puesto administrativo dentro del Servicio Nacional de Salud (NHS), mientras mejoraba su inglés y se preparaba para los exámenes médicos. Con el apoyo de sus colegas del NHS, aprobó los exámenes y volvió a ejercer como doctora en Londres.

**Impacto** – La historia de Inna muestra cómo los refugiados cualificados pueden reconstruir sus vidas y contribuir a sus nuevas comunidades. Ayudó a crear la Ukrainian Medical Charity para apoyar a otros trabajadores sanitarios refugiados y ha influido en un programa más amplio del NHS que ayuda a refugiados a encontrar empleo en el sector sanitario. Más de 60,000 ucranianos trabajan ahora en diferentes sectores en el Reino Unido, demostrando el valor de acoger a los refugiados.

**Próximo paso** – Inna sigue combinando su trabajo médico con la vida familiar mientras apoya a otros profesionales refugiados. Se espera que el Programa de Empleo para Refugiados del NHS crezca, ofreciendo más oportunidades laborales en el sector sanitario en todo el Reino Unido.

**Frase resumen** – Con apoyo y determinación, una doctora ucraniana reconstruyó su carrera en el Reino Unido y ahora ayuda a otros trabajadores sanitarios refugiados a hacer lo mismo.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a stethoscope gently entwined with a blooming sunflower, resting on an open map showing a route from Ukraine to London, all rendered in soft, natural earth tones and muted blues, symbolizing healing, resilience, and a journey toward new roots and hope.
